nodes.name: Every Monday at 9 AM Pacific
nodes.notes: Runs at 9:00 AM every Monday. The workflow timezone is America/Los_Angeles so daylight saving time is handled automatically.

parameters.query: SELECT Id, Name, OwnerId, Status, Engagement_Status__c, IsConverted, IsDeleted, Human_Override__c FROM Lead WHERE Engagement_Status__c = 'Requested Follow Up' AND IsConverted = false AND IsDeleted = false AND Status NOT IN ('Unqualified', 'Closed', 'Disqualified') AND (Human_Override__c = false OR Human_Override__c = null)

nodes.name: Fetch Requested Follow Up Leads
nodes.notes: Fetches only active, non-converted Leads currently in Requested Follow Up. Query errors stop the workflow, preventing task creation from incomplete data.

nodes.name: Fetch Weekly Reminder Tasks
nodes.notes: Runs one batch Task query for all eligible Lead IDs. The no-Leads fallback deliberately returns zero records. Query errors stop the workflow so reminders are not created without a reliable duplicate/completion check.

nodes.name: Determine Eligible Task Reminders
nodes.notes: Groups all matching historical tasks by Lead, calculates hasCompletedTask and hasCurrentMondayTask, and emits one decision per eligible Lead. Only tasks carrying the W15 workflow marker in Description count.

nodes.name: Skip — Missing Lead Owner
nodes.notes: Safe skip: no fallback owner is used. The input item records the Lead ID and missingOwner decision for execution review.

nodes.name: Skip — Completed Reminder Found
nodes.notes: A completed W15 reminder permanently stops future reminders for this Lead under the simplified tracking rule.

nodes.name: Skip — Task Already Created This Monday
nodes.notes: Prevents duplicate W15 tasks when the workflow is retried or manually run again during the same Pacific-time week.

nodes.name: Create Weekly Follow Up Task
nodes.notes: Creates one Not Started, Normal-priority Task due on the current Monday and assigns it to the current Lead Owner. Create errors stop the workflow and remain visible in the execution.

parameters.content:
## Cycle-reset limitation

This simplified W15 intentionally does not write to `Engagement_Status_Tracker__c`. The W14 example proves several tracker fields exist, but it does not provide a reliable field that identifies a new Requested Follow Up cycle or stores a stopped/completed state.

Therefore, any W15 Task with the exact subject, the W15 workflow marker in Description, and `Status = 'Completed'` permanently stops future W15 Tasks for that Lead—even if the Lead later leaves and re-enters Requested Follow Up.

A true reset requires a confirmed Salesforce cycle identifier or status-transition timestamp.

main.node: Skip — Task Already Created This Monday
